cards.back: A simple sorting algorithm that works by dividing the input into a sorted and an unsorted region, and repeatedly moving the smallest element from the unsorted region into the sorted region.
A sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.
A sorting algorithm that selects the smallest element from the unsorted portion of the list and swaps it with the first unsorted element.
O(n^2) in the worst-case scenario, making it impractical for large data sets.
O(1), as it only uses a constant amount of additional space.
